events.reasoning:
NEOW CHOICE ANALYSIS:

Option 0: Enemies in next 3 combats have 1 HP
- This is incredibly strong. Three free fights means:
  - Free card rewards from 3 fights (can pick up Attacks for Nob protection)
  - Free gold from 3 fights
  - Zero HP loss from those fights
  - Effectively lets us path more aggressively in early Act 1

Option 1: Max HP +7 (75 -> 82)
- Small permanent benefit. 7 HP is okay but not exciting.
  
The 1 HP enemies option is far superior. Three free fights is massive tempo - we get card rewards to fix our Attack-light deck (only 3 Attacks in 10 cards) without spending any HP. This is exactly what Defect needs early since the starting deck is so Skill-heavy.

CHOICE: Option 0 - enemies in next 3 combats have 1 HP.

events.translated: [ Enemies in your next three combats have 1 HP ]
events.reasoning: Enemies in next 3 combats have 1 HP - gives free card rewards and gold to fix our Attack-light deck
Leave Neow event
View full Act 1 map for route planning

ACT 1 ROUTE PLANNING:

Boss: Slime Boss
- Slime Boss needs AOE for the split phase
- Defect has some natural AOE (Sweeping Beam, Doom and Gloom evoke)

Map: Two starting paths, both to Monster nodes
- With Neow's Lament (3 charges), first 3 combats are free
- Should path through as many Monster nodes as possible in first 3 floors
- After Lament wears off, we want a rest site before the elite if needed

Deck Status: 4 Attacks, 6 Skills, 0 Powers
- NEED more Attacks for Gremlin Nob protection
- WANT Defragment (Focus is key scaling)
- WANT Attack cards: Ball Lightning, Compile Driver, Sweeping Beam, Doom and Gloom

Strategy: Take path with most monster nodes in first 3 floors to maximize free card rewards. Pick up Attacks aggressively. After Lament runs out, look for rest site or shop before committing to an elite.

Choosing left path (x=1) - either path starts at a Monster which is what we want.
Take left Monster path - Neow Lament makes first 3 combats free, want card rewards
Jaw Worm has 1 HP, Lightning orb passive (3 dmg) will kill at end of turn. No Attacks in hand anyway.
View card reward options

CARD REWARD - Floor 1:
- Claw (0E Attack, common): I believe this deals 3 damage and increases all Claw damage by 2 for the rest of combat. It is an Attack card which we desperately need. 0E cost is great. The scaling only works if you have multiple Claws though, and with just 1 copy it is a weak 3-damage card. BUT it is an Attack (protects vs Nob) and costs 0 energy.
- Reprogram (1E Skill, uncommon): I am not sure what this does. It might lose Focus and gain Strength/Dexterity? That seems anti-synergistic with orb strategy. Skipping since I do not know it well enough.
- Leap (1E Skill, common): 9 block for 1E. Solid block card but we already have 4 Defends. Adding another Skill makes Nob even worse.

Analysis: Claw is the pick. It is an Attack card (Nob protection), costs 0 energy (always playable), and while the scaling is weak with 1 copy, it is the only Attack option here. We need to shift our Attack/Skill ratio.

TAKE: Claw
Take Claw - 0E Attack, helps fix our Attack-light deck for Nob protection
Proceeding
Take Monster path - Neow Lament still has 2 charges, free fight
Dualcast evokes Lightning orb twice (8 dmg each to random enemy). First evoke kills one louse (1 HP), second evoke kills the other. Clean instant kill on both.
Dualcast evokes Lightning twice, kills both 1 HP louses
Take Swift Potion - draw cards is always useful
View card reward
All 3 options are Skills. Deck is already 7 Skills vs 4 Attacks (after adding Claw). Adding more Skills makes Gremlin Nob even worse. Skip.
Proceeding
Only path available - Unknown room, might be event/fight/merchant

SHINING LIGHT EVENT:
- Enter: Upgrade 2 random cards, lose 15 HP (75 -> 60)
- Leave: Nothing

At 75/75 HP, losing 15 HP to 60 is very manageable (80% HP).
Upgrading 2 random cards is equivalent to 2 campfire upgrades - extremely valuable.
Even if the upgrades hit Strikes/Defends, upgraded Strikes do 9 damage and upgraded Defends give 8 block.
Best case: hits Zap (0E, no change?) or Dualcast (becomes 0E!) or Claw.

The HP cost is real but we still have 1 Neow Lament charge, so next fight is free anyway.

TAKE IT.
Enter Shining Light - 2 free upgrades for 15 HP at full health is excellent value
Leave event
Take Monster - last Neow Lament charge makes this fight free, guaranteed card reward

Proceeding
Only path available - Monster node. Neow Lament is used up, this is our first real fight.

FIGHT STRATEGY - Looter (45 HP):
- Turns 1-2: Mug (10 dmg + steals gold)
- Turn 3+: Lunge (12 dmg) or Smoke Bomb (block), then Escape
- Must kill by Turn 3-4 to prevent escape

TURN 1 PLAN:
INCOMING: 10 damage (Mug)
Hand: Defend+ (1E, 8 block), Strike x3 (1E each, 6 dmg), Strike+ (1E, 9 dmg)
Lightning orb passive: 3 dmg at end of turn

PATHS TO ZERO DAMAGE:
- Path A: Defend+ (8 block) + 2x Strike (12 dmg) + Lightning passive (3 dmg) = 8 block vs 10 incoming = take 2 damage. 15 total damage dealt. Not zero.
- Path B: Strike+ + 2x Strike (9+6+6=21 dmg) + Lightning (3) = 24 damage, but take full 10 damage. 45-24=21 HP left.
- Path C: Defend+ + Strike+ + Strike = 8 block (take 2) + 15 damage + 3 passive = 18 total damage. 

I cannot achieve zero damage with this hand. Defend+ only gives 8 vs 10 incoming.
Best approach: maximize damage to kill fast. 

Path B: Play 3 Strikes and Strike+ for max damage:
Strike+ (9) + Strike (6) + Strike (6) = 21 damage + 3 Lightning passive = 24 damage dealt. Take 10.
Leaves Looter at 45-24 = 21 HP.

Path C: Defend+ + Strike+ + Strike = 15 + 3 = 18 damage, take 2. Looter at 27 HP.
Save 8 HP but deal 6 less damage. Looter lives longer, steals more gold.

I think going all-in on damage (Path B) is better. Taking 10 HP (60->50) but having Looter at 21 HP means we can likely kill Turn 2. Defending extends the fight.

CHOSEN: Path B - Strike+ + Strike + Strike, take the 10 damage.

events.command: play Strike+ 0; play Strike 0; play Strike 0; end
events.translated: Strike+ → Looter → Strike → Looter → Strike → Looter → End Turn
events.reasoning: All-in on damage: 9+6+6=21 + 3 Lightning passive = 24 total. Looter to 21 HP. Take 10 dmg to kill fast and prevent escape.

LOOTER TURN 2:
INCOMING: 10 damage (Mug, steals more gold)
Hand: 3x Defend (5 block each), Dualcast (1E, evoke front orb twice), Zap (1E, channel Lightning)
Orbs: Lightning(3/8)
Draw pile: just Claw

PATHS:
- Path A: 2x Defend (10 block) + Dualcast (evoke Lightning twice = 8+8=16 dmg) + Lightning passive... wait, if I evoke the orb, I lose it. Dualcast evokes front orb twice. Front orb is Lightning. Evoke = 8 damage each = 16 damage to random enemy (only 1 enemy so guaranteed). But I lose the Lightning orb after evoke. Then passive = 0 (no orbs left). Total: 16 damage, 10 block = zero damage taken! Looter goes to 21-16=5 HP.

- Path B: 2x Defend (10 block) + Zap (channel 2nd Lightning) = 0 block extra. 10 block = zero damage. Lightning passives at end = 3+3 = 6 damage. Looter to 21-6=15 HP.

- Path C: Defend + Dualcast + Zap = 5 block (take 5) + 16 evoke + channel new Lightning. Passive = 3 at end. Total: 19 damage. Looter to 21-19=2 HP. Take 5 damage.

Path A is the best: zero damage taken AND 16 damage dealt (Looter to 5 HP). Next turn we kill easily.

Wait, actually: 2x Defend (10 block, 2E) + Dualcast (1E) = 3E total. That works!
After Dualcast evokes Lightning twice, the orb is consumed. No passive at end of turn. But we dealt 16 evoke damage.

CHOSEN: Path A - Defend, Defend, Dualcast. Zero damage, Looter to 5 HP.

events.command: play Defend 0; play Defend 0; play Dualcast; end
events.translated: Defend → Looter → Defend → Looter → Dualcast → End Turn
events.reasoning: 2x Defend (10 block) blocks all 10 incoming. Dualcast evokes Lightning twice (8+8=16 dmg). Looter to 5 HP. Zero damage taken.
Claw (3 dmg, 0E) kills the 5 HP Looter. No need to play more cards.
Strike kills the 2 HP Looter. Defend intent so no incoming damage.
View card reward

CARD REWARD - Floor 5:
- Cold Snap (1E Attack, common): I think this deals 6 damage and channels 1 Frost orb. It is an Attack card (Nob safe!) that also provides defense via Frost orb passive block. Excellent dual-purpose card.
- Creative AI (3E Power, rare): Generates a random Power each turn. At 3E cost it is very expensive for Act 1 where we have only 3 energy. I think this is a late-game card that does not help us now.
- Rebound (1E Attack, common): I think this deals 9 damage and puts the next card played on top of draw pile. Decent damage, interesting utility.

Cold Snap is the clear best pick:
1. It is an Attack (helps vs Nob)
2. It channels Frost orbs (passive block = defense scaling)
3. 6 damage is solid
4. Helps build toward Frost+Focus defensive strategy

TAKE: Cold Snap
Take Cold Snap - Attack that channels Frost orb. Dual purpose offense+defense, safe vs Nob.
Proceeding

ELITE APPROACH - Floor 6:
Only path is an Elite. At 38/75 HP (50%), this is risky but unavoidable.

Deck: 6 Attacks, 6 Skills (balanced)
Potions: Swift Potion (draw 3), Speed Potion (5 Dex for 1 turn)
Key cards: Cold Snap (Attack + Frost), Claw (0E Attack), Strike+ (9 dmg), Defend+ (8 block)

Elite matchups:
- Gremlin Nob: 6 Attacks is okay. Avoid playing Skills when possible. Claw (0E, Attack) is great here. Cold Snap is Attack so safe. Use Swift Potion to find more Attacks. Kill fast.
- Lagavulin: Comes out of sleep after 3 turns or if attacked. Use setup turns to channel orbs with Zap. Then burst with Dualcast. Speed Potion for a big block turn.
- Sentries: AOE fight, we lack AOE. Daze cards clog deck. This is the worst matchup.

Use potions aggressively - this is survival.
Only path available. Elite at 38/75 HP is risky but we have 2 potions and a balanced deck.

FIGHT STRATEGY - Gremlin Nob (85 HP):
Nob starts with Bellow (Enrage 2) - Turn 1 is FREE, no damage incoming.
After that: Rush (14 dmg, 67%) or Skull Bash (6 dmg + Vulnerable, 33%).

KEY RULE: Play ZERO Skills. Every Skill triggers Enrage = +2 Str permanently.
My deck: 6 Attacks (4 Strike, Claw, Cold Snap), 6 Skills (4 Defend, Zap, Dualcast)
In a 5-card hand, expect 2-3 Attacks on average.

TURN 1 (Bellow - FREE turn):
Hand: Strike, Zap, Strike+, Dualcast, Defend+
Attacks available: Strike (6), Strike+ (9) = 2 Attacks for 15 damage. Cost: 2E.
Skills in hand: Zap, Dualcast, Defend+ - DO NOT PLAY ANY.
Lightning orb passive: 3 damage at end of turn.
Total T1 damage: 15 + 3 = 18 damage. Nob to 85-18 = 67 HP.

Should I play Dualcast for 16 burst (evoke Lightning twice)? NO - it is a Skill, triggers Enrage.
1E leftover with nothing safe to play. Just play the 2 Attacks.

Potions plan:
- Swift Potion (draw 3): Use early to find more Attacks. But it does not help if drawn cards are Skills.
- Speed Potion (5 Dex): Save for a turn when we are forced to play Defend/block Skills.

Actually wait: Use Swift Potion Turn 1 to draw 3 more cards, hoping for Attacks. Swift Potion is not a Skill play, it is a potion use.

Updated T1 plan: Strike (1E) + Strike+ (1E) + Swift Potion (draw 3 from draw pile: Defend, Defend, Defend, Claw, Cold Snap, Strike, Strike). 7 cards in draw, draw 3. Good chance of drawing Attack cards.

Swift Potion then play all Attacks drawn.

CHOSEN: Swift Potion -> Strike + Strike+ + any Attacks drawn from potion.
Use Swift Potion on free turn to draw 3 more cards, hoping for Attacks vs Nob

events.reasoning: Free turn (Bellow). Play all 4 Attacks: Claw(3)+Strike(6)+Strike+(9)+Cold Snap(6)=24 dmg + 3 Lightning passive = 27. Nob to 58. Cold Snap channels Frost for future block. Zero Skills played.

NOB TURN 2:
INCOMING: 14 damage (Rush)
Hand: Defend, Strike, Strike, Defend, Strike+
Orbs: Lightning (3 passive dmg), Frost (2 passive block)

PATHS:
- Path A (No Skills): Strike + Strike + Strike+ = 6+6+9 = 21 damage. Frost passive = 2 block. Take 14-2 = 12 damage. HP: 38-12 = 26. Nob to 58-21-3(lightning) = 34 HP.
- Path B (1 Defend): Defend + Strike + Strike+ = 5 block + 15 damage. Take 14-5-2(frost) = 7 damage. HP: 38-7 = 31. But Nob gains +2 Str from Defend = attacks for 16 next turn. Nob to 58-15-3 = 40 HP.
- Path C (Speed Potion + no Skills): Strike + Strike + Strike+ = 21 damage. Speed Potion (5 Dex) gives Frost passive... wait, Dex only applies to block from cards, not orbs. Speed Potion adds 5 block to each Defend played. Not useful if playing 0 Skills. Actually does Dex affect orb block? I don't think so.

Path A is correct: take the 12 damage, play all 3 Attacks, no Skills. 
26 HP after this turn. Nob at 34 HP. Still needs ~2 more turns to kill.

CHOSEN: Strike + Strike + Strike+ for 21 damage, take 12.

events.command: play Strike 0; play Strike 0; play Strike+ 0; end
events.translated: Strike → Gremlin Nob → Strike → Gremlin Nob → Strike+ → Gremlin Nob → End Turn
events.reasoning: 3 Attacks for 21 dmg, no Skills. Take 14-2(Frost)=12 damage. Nob to ~34 HP.

NOB TURN 3:
INCOMING: 14 damage (Rush again)
HP: 26. If I take 14, I go to 12 HP (minus Frost 2 block = take 12, go to 14 HP).
Hand: Zap (Skill), Defend (Skill), Cold Snap (Attack!), Dualcast (Skill), Defend+ (Skill)
Draw pile: Claw, Strike

CRITICAL: Only 1 Attack in hand (Cold Snap, 6 dmg). Nob has 34 HP.
Cold Snap (6) + Lightning passive (3) = 9 damage. Nob to 25 HP.
Frost passive = 2 block. Take 14-2 = 12 damage. HP: 26-12 = 14.

Can I kill with Dualcast? Dualcast evokes front orb (Lightning) twice = 8+8 = 16 damage. But Dualcast is a Skill = +2 Str for Nob. 
Total with Dualcast: Cold Snap (6) + Dualcast evoke (16) = 22 damage + Lightning... wait, Dualcast evokes the FRONT orb. My orbs are Lightning, Frost, empty. Dualcast evokes Lightning twice = 16 damage. But then Lightning orb is gone. 
After Dualcast: no Lightning passive. Cold Snap channels Frost (but it was played before Dualcast? Let me think about order).

If I play Cold Snap first: 6 dmg, channels Frost. Orbs become: Lightning, Frost, Frost.
Then Dualcast: evokes front orb (Lightning) twice = 8+8 = 16 damage to Nob.
Orbs after: Frost, Frost.
Total: 6 + 16 = 22 damage. Frost passive = 2+2 = 4 block. Nob goes to 34-22 = 12 HP.
Take 14-4 = 10 damage. HP: 26-10 = 16 HP.
But Nob gains +2 Str from Dualcast (Skill) = attacks for 16 next turn.

Next turn: need to do 12 damage with Nob attacking for 16+. Draw pile is Claw (3) and Strike (6) = only 9 damage! Plus we reshuffle.

Alternative: Cold Snap only (6 dmg, 1E). Save 2E. Don't play Skills. Take 12 dmg (26-12=14 HP).
Nob to 34-6-3(lightning passive) = 25 HP.
Next turn: full reshuffle, draw 5 from 11 cards. Expect 2-3 Attacks. Should be able to do 15-20 damage.
But Nob still at Str 0, attacks for 14. Take 14 minus Frost (2) = 12 again. 14-12 = 2 HP??

That is too close to death. I need to use Speed Potion and play Defends.

Actually let me reconsider: Speed Potion gives 5 Dexterity for 1 turn. If I play Defend (5+5=10 block) + Defend+ (8+5=13 block) that is 23 block against 14 incoming = zero damage! But that is 2 Skills = +4 Str for Nob.

Or: Speed Potion + Defend+ only (13 block vs 14 = take 1) + Cold Snap (6 dmg) + save 1E. 1 Skill = +2 Str.

Best plan: Speed Potion + Cold Snap + Defend+ 
- Block: 13 (Defend+ with Dex 5) + 2 (Frost) = 15 vs 14 incoming = zero damage!
- Damage: Cold Snap 6 + Lightning passive 3 = 9
- Nob gains +2 Str (from Defend+), attacks for 16 next turn
- Nob to 34-9 = 25 HP
- Cost: 2E, 1E left. Could play Zap (another Skill +2 Str) or save.
  
Wait, I have 1E left. If I play Dualcast (Skill, +2 Str, total +4): evoke Lightning twice = 16 dmg. Nob to 25-16 = 9 HP! But +4 Str total means 18 damage next turn.

Hmm, Cold Snap (6) + Dualcast (16) + Lightning... wait, after Dualcast evokes Lightning, no Lightning passive. But Cold Snap adds Frost. So passives: Frost (2 block) only.

Revised: Speed Potion + Defend+ (13 block) + Cold Snap (6 dmg) + Dualcast (16 dmg evoke). 
Block: 13 + 2 (Frost from Cold Snap passive? Actually after Dualcast evokes Lightning, orbs are Frost, Frost. Each gives 2 passive block = 4 block.) 
Total block: 13 + 4 = 17 vs 14 = ZERO damage.
Damage: 6 + 16 = 22. Nob to 34-22 = 12 HP.
Skills played: Defend+ and Dualcast = +4 Str. Nob attacks for 18 next turn.
3E used: Defend+(1) + Cold Snap(1) + Dualcast(1) = 3E. Perfect.

Next turn at 26 HP: need 12 damage to kill. Full reshuffle. Nob attacks for 18.

Actually... let me try an aggressive approach:
Cold Snap (6, 1E) + Dualcast (evoke Lightning 2x = 16, 1E Skill) = 22 damage. Nob to 12 HP.
Zap (0E Skill) = channel Lightning. +2 Str for Nob.
But wait, Zap is 1E not 0E. Let me check... the hand says Zap (skill, 1E). Hmm, base Zap costs 0E. But it shows 1E here. Let me not assume.

OK, looking at hand: Zap (skill, 1E). So Zap costs 1E in this game.

Plan: Cold Snap (1E) + Dualcast (1E) + Defend+ (1E) = 3E. 
Speed Potion for 5 Dex. Defend+ gives 8+5=13 block.
After Dualcast: orbs = Frost, Frost (from Cold Snap). Passive = 2+2 = 4 block.
Total block: 13+4 = 17 > 14. Zero damage taken!
Total damage: 6+16 = 22. Nob to 12 HP. 2 Skills played = +4 Str.

CHOSEN: Speed Potion + Cold Snap + Dualcast + Defend+. Zero damage, Nob to 12 HP.
Speed Potion for 5 Dex - makes Defend+ give 13 block for zero damage turn

CARD REWARD - Floor 6 (Elite):
- Rebound (1E Attack): 9 damage, next card goes on top of draw pile. Decent but nothing special.
- Capacitor (1E Power): Adds orb slots (I think +2). Powers are great vs Nob (don't trigger Enrage). More orb slots = more passive triggers. With Emotion Chip, this is amazing - more orbs = more double triggers. Also it is a Power so it does not trigger Nob Enrage.
- Double Energy (1E Skill): Doubles current energy? Not sure.

Capacitor synergizes incredibly well with Emotion Chip and our Frost orb strategy. More orb slots means each Frost orb stays around longer for passive block. This is an easy take.

TAKE: Capacitor
Take Capacitor - adds orb slots, synergizes with Emotion Chip for double passive triggers. Key scaling card for Defect.
Proceeding

MAP DECISION - Floor 7:
Options: Monster (x=0) or Rest (x=2)
HP: 25/75 (33%) - CRITICAL

At 33% HP, we desperately need healing. A hallway fight at 25 HP could kill us if we take 15+ damage. The rest site heals 30% = 22 HP, bringing us to 47/75.

Normally I would consider upgrading at rest sites (upgrade is default), but at 33% HP we must heal. This is exactly the scenario where rest > upgrade.

CHOICE: Rest site (heal).
Rest site - at 25/75 HP (33%) we must heal. Too low for another fight.
Heal 22 HP at rest site. 25->47 HP. Survival priority over upgrade.
Proceeding
Only path - Monster node. At 47/75 HP, should be manageable.

FIGHT STRATEGY - Slaver (47 HP):
No playbook entry. I think Slavers attack for ~12-14 damage with debuffs.
Intent says DEBUG so I cannot see the incoming damage number.

Hand: Strike (1E), Defend (1E), Defend (1E), Defend+ (1E), Capacitor (1E Power)
Only 1 Attack in hand.

TURN 1 PLAN:
Since I do not know the incoming damage, I should play defensively.
Capacitor is a Power that adds orb slots - great to play early since it compounds value.
Strike (6 dmg) + Capacitor (1E Power, adds orb slots) + Defend (5 block) = 3E.
Or: Strike + Capacitor + Defend+ (8 block) = 3E. Better block.

Actually, playing Capacitor Turn 1 is high value - it gives us more orb slots for the rest of combat. Then we can fill them with Frost/Lightning for passive defense.

events.reasoning: Entangled - cant play Attacks. Zap channels Lightning (passive 3 dmg kills 4 HP Slaver at end of turn). 2x Defend (10 block) vs 8 incoming = zero damage.

CARD REWARD - Floor 8:
- Coolheaded (1E Skill): Channel Frost + Draw 1. Excellent cantrip. BUT it is a Skill - adding another Skill to the deck means more Nob triggers. However, we already beat Nob. Future Nobs are possible but the Frost orb + draw is very strong value. With Capacitor giving us 5 orb slots and Emotion Chip for double passives, channeling Frost is especially valuable.
- Go for the Eyes (0E Attack): I believe this is 3 damage + apply Weak if enemy is attacking. 0E is great, and it is an Attack. Weak reduces enemy damage by 25%. Very useful defensively.
- Bullseye (1E Attack): I think this deals damage and applies Lock-On (enemies take more orb damage). With our orb-heavy strategy this could be strong.

Analysis: We already beat Nob so the Skill concern is reduced. Coolheaded is one of the best Defect common cards - Frost orb + cantrip is amazing cycle value. With 5 orb slots (Capacitor) and Emotion Chip, getting Frost orbs into slots is high priority.

BUT Go for the Eyes at 0E is very efficient - free Attack that applies Weak.

I think Coolheaded is the stronger pick for our orb strategy. It helps fill our 5 orb slots with Frost for passive block.

TAKE: Coolheaded
Take Coolheaded - Frost orb + Draw 1 cantrip. Fills our 5 orb slots with Frost for passive block. Synergizes with Capacitor and Emotion Chip.
Proceeding
Treasure room - free relic!
Collect 23 gold

events.text: PLAN — Combat: vs Fungi Beast + Spike Slime (M)

events.summary: Combat: vs Fungi Beast + Spike Slime (M)

events.reasoning:
FIGHT STRATEGY - Fungi Beast (28 HP) + Spike Slime M (28 HP):
- Fungi Beast scales with Grow (+3 Str). Kill fast.
- Spike Slime M: I think it attacks for 8-16 damage and adds Slimed to discard.
- When Fungi Beast dies: Spore Cloud 2 = applies 2 Vulnerable.

Priority: Kill Fungi Beast first (it scales). But be careful of Spore Cloud Vulnerability + Slime attack on same turn.

TURN 1:
Vigor 8 (Akabeko) - first Attack deals +8 damage.
Hand: Coolheaded (1E Skill), 2x Strike (1E each), Zap (1E Skill), Dualcast (1E Skill)
3 orb slots (Capacitor adds slots in combat? No, Capacitor is in deck not yet played.)

Wait - orbs show 3 slots. Capacitor has not been played yet this combat. Need to play it.

First Attack gets Vigor 8. Strike + 8 = 14 damage.

Plan: Coolheaded (1E, Frost orb + draw 1) -> drawn card may be useful -> Strike (1E, 14 dmg with Vigor) on Fungi Beast -> Strike (1E, 6 dmg) on Fungi Beast.
Total on Fungi Beast: 14+6 = 20 damage. It has 28 HP, goes to 8.
Lightning passive: 3 to random = could hit either.

BUT Coolheaded draws a card. DRAW RULE: never include cards after draw in turn sequence.
So I should play Coolheaded LAST or separately.

Alternative: Strike (14 dmg, Vigor) + Strike (6 dmg) on Fungi Beast + Zap (channel Lightning) = 3E.
20 damage on Fungi Beast. Lightning passives: 2x Lightning at end = 6 random damage. Fungi Beast could go to 28-20=8, then 3-6 random damage.

Actually wait: Zap channels Lightning, so I would have 2 Lightning orbs. Each deals 3 passive at end = 6 total random damage across both enemies.

Better: Strike (14 Vigor) on Fungi Beast + Strike (6) on Fungi Beast + Coolheaded (Frost + draw 1). 
But Coolheaded draws! Rule says do not include cards after draw. So Coolheaded must be last in sequence.

CHOSEN: Strike (14 with Vigor) on Fungi Beast 0, Strike (6) on Fungi Beast 0, Coolheaded last. 3E exactly. 20 damage to Fungi Beast, Frost orb, draw 1.

events.command: play Strike 0; play Cold Snap 1; play Defend 0; end

events.reasoning: Strike kills Fungi Beast (6>5 HP). Cold Snap on Spike Slime (6 dmg + Frost orb). Defend (3 block from Frail) for safety. Fungi dies before attacking, Spike debuffs = 0 incoming damage expected.
Spike Slime debuffing = no damage. Play Defend for 3 block (Frail). 1E left.

SPIKE SLIME TURN 3:
INCOMING: 12 + I am Vulnerable = 18 damage (50% more)

events.reasoning:
Neow options analysis for Defect A0:

[0] Remove a Card -- Removing a Strike or Defend thins the deck slightly. Decent but not game-changing.

[1] Obtain 100 Gold -- 199 total gold, could buy a strong card from shop early. Good flexibility.

[2] Take 21 damage, Choose a rare Card -- 54/75 HP after. Rare cards for Defect include Biased Cognition (incredibly strong), Electrodynamics, Creative AI, etc. Being able to CHOOSE means we can pick the best one. 21 damage is significant (28% HP) but a rare card can define the run. I think this is high risk/high reward.

[3] Lose Cracked Core, Obtain random boss Relic -- Cracked Core channels 1 Lightning at start of combat. It's decent but not amazing. Boss relics can be game-changing (Snecko Eye, Inserter, Nuclear Battery) or bad (Runic Dome, Tiny House). This is a gamble.

My assessment:
- Option 2 (rare card choice) is probably the strongest if we get offered Biased Cognition, Defragment, or Electrodynamics. But 21 damage is risky early.
- Option 3 (boss relic swap) is the greediest play. Cracked Core is okay but replaceable. Boss relics can carry runs.
- Option 0 (card remove) is safe but boring.
- Option 1 (100 gold) is solid value.

I think Option 3 (boss relic swap) is the best play here. Cracked Core gives us 1 Lightning per fight which is nice but not critical. Boss relics have a very high ceiling. At A0, we have some room to be greedy. The worst outcomes (Runic Dome, Ectoplasm) are manageable.

Actually, wait -- Option 2 lets us CHOOSE a rare. That's very powerful. If Biased Cognition or Electrodynamics shows up, it could define the whole run. 21 damage means 54 HP but at A0 hallway fights should be manageable. 

I'll go with Option 3 (boss relic swap). The upside is higher and more consistent than Option 2. Boss relics provide value every single fight for the entire run.

events.translated: [ Lose your starting Relic Obtain a random boss Re
events.reasoning: Swap Cracked Core for random boss relic. Boss relics provide run-defining value every fight. Cracked Core is replaceable. At A0 we can afford to be greedy.

Pandora Box deck analysis:

ATTACKS (3): Barrage (1E), Melter (1E), Sweeping Beam (1E)
SKILLS (7): Chill (0E), Coolheaded (1E), Dualcast (1E), Overclock (0E), Seek (0E), Seek (0E), Zap (1E)

This is... interesting. Very skill-heavy still (7 skills vs 3 attacks). Average cost is incredibly low at 0.6E.

Good cards I recognize:
- Sweeping Beam: AOE attack + draw. Good!
- Barrage: Deals damage per orb. Great with orbs up.
- Coolheaded: Channels Frost + draw. Good defense and cycle.
- Overclock: 0-cost, draw cards. Great cycle.
- Seek: 0-cost, search deck for a card. Very powerful for consistency.
- Two Seeks is amazing -- we can always find what we need.
- Chill: 0-cost, channels Frost. Good defensive option.
- Zap: Channel Lightning. Standard.
- Dualcast: Evoke frontmost orb twice. Standard.

Cards I am less sure about:
- Melter: I think it deals damage and removes block from an enemy? Need to check.

Concerns:
- Only 3 attacks. Gremlin Nob will DESTROY us if we face him -- he gets stronger when we play skills.
- No block cards at all! Zero Defends replaced. We rely entirely on Frost orbs for defense.
- No Focus cards yet. Need Defragment badly.

Priorities:
1. MUST get attack cards early for Nob insurance
2. Need Focus (Defragment, Biased Cognition)
3. Avoid Nob elite path if possible
4. The Seeks make this deck very consistent -- we can find the right card when needed

Overall this is a decent Pandora result. The 0-cost cards (Seek x2, Chill, Overclock) give us great energy efficiency. But we NEED attacks.

Lagavulin elite! 110 HP, Metallicize 8, currently sleeping. Strategy: I should wake it ASAP because sleeping = 8 block per turn that I have to get through. When woken, Metallicize is removed! Pattern after waking: Attack(18) -> Attack(18) -> Siphon Soul(-1 Str/-1 Dex). The Siphon Soul debuffs are permanent and devastating. Need to kill fast. My options: 1) Attack now to wake it (loses Metallicize 8 but it attacks next turn for 18). 2) Wait 3 turns (it auto-wakes and attacks). Waiting means 3 turns of 8 block from Metallicize to deal with. Better to wake it NOW since we have good damage and Metallicize removal is huge. Plan: Use Cultist Potion for +1 Ritual (scaling Str). Attack with everything. Melter might strip its 8 block! Turn 1: Wake it up, deal max damage. If stunned for 1 turn after waking, thats a free damage turn. Turn 1 plan: Cultist Potion (Ritual 1 = +1 Str per turn cumulative). Claw (0E, free). Overclock (0E, draw 2). Melter (1E, 10 dmg + strips block?). Doom and Gloom (2E, 10 dmg + Dark orb). Thats 3E. Or Zap (1E) + Melter (1E) + Claw (0E) + Overclock (0E) + something. Let me think about energy. 3E total. Doom and Gloom is 2E. Melter is 1E. Thats 3E for 20 damage. Or Melter (1E) + Zap (1E) + something 1E = 3E. Overclock and Claw are free. So: Overclock (0E, draw 2), Claw (0E), Melter (1E), then 2E remaining... Actually let me use Cultist Potion first for scaling.

Gremlin Nob Turn 1 (FREE - Bellow, no Enrage yet). Play ALL skills now. Hand: Chill(0E), Seek(0E), Sweeping Beam(1E), Doom and Gloom(2E), Barrage(1E). Skills: Chill + Seek. Plan: Seek -> Capacitor -> Chill -> then attacks. After Seek gets Capacitor (1E), play Capacitor (1E), Chill (0E) = 1 Frost. Then 2E left: Sweeping Beam (1E, 6 damage) + Barrage (1E, but only 1 orb = 4 damage). Or Doom and Gloom (2E, ~10+8 Vigor = 18 damage + Dark orb). Doom and Gloom uses all remaining energy but deals most damage and uses Vigor 8. After Capacitor: 5 orb slots. Chill channels 1 Frost. Then Doom and Gloom: 10+8=18 damage + Dark orb = 2 orbs total. Passive: 1 Frost * 3 block + Dark accumulates. OR I could do Sweeping Beam (1E, 6+8=14 damage + draw 1) then Barrage (1E, 4*2=8 damage if I have 2 orbs from Chill Frost + Sweeping Beam draw doesnt channel). Actually Vigor only applies to first attack. So Doom and Gloom first (2E, 18 damage), consuming Vigor, vs Sweeping Beam first (1E, 14 damage) then Barrage (1E, uses remaining Vigor... no Vigor is consumed on first attack). Let me maximize: play Doom and Gloom first for 18 damage (10+8 Vigor) on 2E. Channels Dark orb. Then no energy for other attacks. Total: 18 damage + Dark orb + 1 Frost. Or: Sweeping Beam (14 damage + draw) then something with 1E. 14 > 10 base but Doom and Gloom is 18 with Vigor. Best: Doom and Gloom for 18 damage. But wait, should I Seek for Coolheaded+ instead of Capacitor? Coolheaded+ is a skill that draws 2 + channels Frost. I can play it turn 1 safely. Then Id have Coolheaded+(1E) + Chill(0E) = 2 Frost, 2E for Doom and Gloom (18 dmg). 2 Frost passive = 6 block. Or Capacitor gives 5 slots = more Frost capacity. I think Capacitor is better long-term since I need slots for orbs. Plan: Seek->Capacitor, Chill(0E), Capacitor(1E), Doom and Gloom(2E, 18 dmg).

Loop (1E, power, uncommon): At the start of your turn, trigger the passive ability of your front orb.

This means the front orb triggers passive TWICE each turn (once from Loop at start, once from normal end-of-turn passive). 

With my Frost-heavy deck, if Frost is in front slot:
- Normal Frost passive: 2+Focus = 3 block per Frost orb
- Loop adds: extra 3 block from front Frost orb specifically
- Net gain: +3 block per turn (with Focus 1)

With Lightning in front:
- Normal Lightning passive: 3+Focus = 4 random damage
- Loop adds: extra 4 random damage from front Lightning
- Net: +4 damage per turn

This is decent scaling. With 5 orb slots (Capacitor), this is solid. Adding another power also synergizes with Mummified Hand (random card becomes free when playing a power).

However, it adds a card to the deck (now 21 cards). My deck is already quite lean and effective. Adding Loop is marginal improvement - 3 extra block or 4 extra damage per turn. Not game-changing but not bad either.

I think I should take it. Extra passive triggers are good, especially for long fights like The Champ. And it's another Power for Mummified Hand synergy.

Loop triggers front orb passive an extra time per turn. With Frost-heavy deck, adds ~3 block/turn. Also another Power for Mummified Hand synergy. Worth taking for scaling in longer fights like The Champ.
